Video Games.body: My shop is named 'Yaki Nomi Balls'
My favorite is We Love Katamari.
I have only played a few hours. I like the magick abilities, but I haven't had the itch to play more.
I have played many hours, and I love co-op. I have not beaten the grim reapear but I have witnessed it in the flesh.
I am almost to the moon.
I mostly like watching this one.
I also mostly like watching this one.
I have almost completed the game.
LeafGreen, Emerald, Sapphire, FireRed, Mystery Dungeon, White, Soulsilver, X/Y, and some of Sword. And Pokopia. HEHE.

03/2025.subheader: Today I figured out how to automate these logs from google docs > google sheets > website. I’ve been piecing it together and finally got the front-end to work.
03/2025.body: I <3 JSON!!! Thank you, JSON!!! Now everytime I update this document it will push it automatically to my website. Isn’t that amazing?

03/2025.subheader: Today my breakout boards for the PCM1794A DAC and opamp OPA1612 are supposed to come. But I don’t think they will. They haven’t been shipped out yet. Here is my shopping list for the electronics store:
03/2025.body:
- 5V to 3.3V buck converter/step-down converter
- Block terminals
- Breadboards
- 22 AWG gauge wire
- AC plugin wire

03/2025.subheader: I got my breakout boards for the pcm1794a DAC and the opa1612 opamp. I have to solder them. I have soldered small components before, but nothing as small as these. I need to go to the store and see if they have:
03/2025.body:
- Solder flux
- Solder
- Fine tip for a soldering gun
- A regulated soldering gun with a temp gauge
- Another breadboard
- Capacitors

03/2025.subheader: I successfully de-soldered two XLR ports from the preamp.
03/2025.body: I used my old $15 iron that is a pathetic nub. De-soldering components is addicting. As soon as I go home today I’m going to do as many more parts as I can before I become dizzily unable.

03/2025.subheader: I saw a bald man in a barber shop getting his head shined.
03/2025.body: This was definitely the first time I’ve seen this occurrence live. The image lasted only a moment, but in my mind it will last a long time, I’m sure.

03/2025.subheader:
Last night I started a new save of Pokemon FireRed. I played through all the games of this series except this one.
My current team (in this order) is:
03/2025.body: Mankey, Weedle, Wartortle , and Clefairy. I don’t like any of them. I’m hoping to encounter a better one soon.

05/2026.subheader: The parts have arrived. It's time to assemble.
05/2026.body:
I have plans to livestream and assemble, while my friends play a DJ set. I am still on my Factorio grind, but I'm secretly hoping my friend's interest falls of so I can focus on making things again. Even though it is my own volition that can create things for myself, I am extremely influenced and will pursue even the littlest hint of someone else's curiosities. Games like Factorio are especially dangerous because it seems like it scales endlessly. 

I want the clout of finishing the Space Age DLC, or even getting to the point where I can start it. I won't be upset if I continue playing. I am pacing myself well in terms of project management, but I could be having more productive nighttime activities. My media consumption is down - which isn't necessarily a bad thing, but I haven't read any type of written media in a long time. 

I want to improve my skills in 3D printing - I have an old delta printer that a coworker/friend let me borrow, but there are some .gcode issues that I have been avoidant in confronting. I tried printing a blue shell. There were explicit errors on the printer that I ignored in blind hope of it being fine. It was an error that was outside my range of knowledge - so I ignored it. Not the best problem-solving mindset, but I also wanted to see how the printer ran with baseline input. 

I'll return to it soon by the end of the week. I want to print some dolls.
